description: Physical print media, such as books, magazines, or manuals.
Analog videotape format used mainly in the 1980s and 1990s for movies and home recordings.
Digital optical disc storage format commonly used for movies, software, and data storage.
High-definition optical disc format offering higher storage capacity than DVD, often used for HD movies.
Nintendo Entertainment System cartridges used for 8-bit video games.
Super Nintendo Entertainment System cartridges used for 16-bit video games.
Nintendo 64 cartridges used for 3D video games in the late 1990s.
Nintendo GameCube mini optical discs used for video games.
Nintendo Wii optical discs, backward compatible with GameCube discs.
Nintendo Wii U proprietary high-density discs used for video games.
Nintendo Switch game cards, a cartridge-like format for modern portable and console gaming.
Nintendo Switch 2 game cards, a cartridge-like format for modern portable and console gaming.
PlayStation 1 compact discs used for video games and multimedia.
PlayStation 2 game console discs, often used for video games and DVD movie playback.
PlayStation 3 game console discs, often used for video games and Blu-ray movie playback.
PlayStation 4 game console discs, often used for video games and Blu-ray movie playback.
PlayStation 5 game console discs, often used for video games and Ultra HD Blu-ray movies.
Original Xbox game console discs, used for video games and some multimedia.
Xbox 360 game console discs, typically used for video games and multimedia.
Xbox One game console discs, often used for video games and Blu-ray playback.
Xbox Series X game console discs, often used for video games and Ultra HD Blu-ray movies.
Non-physical format distributed via the internet, including downloads and streaming services.
